title: Corpay, Inc. (CPAY) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Corpay, Inc. (CPAY).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 9% free cash flow growth rate reflects Corpay's strong historical performance and its strategic pivot towards comprehensive corporate payments, which provides a massive total addressable market for expansion beyond traditional fleet cards.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate is applied, balancing the company's highly sticky B2B customer base and strong cash generation against regulatory risks and vulnerability to commercial macro downturns.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3% terminal growth rate is appropriate, slightly above baseline inflation, recognizing the inherent stickiness and long-term pricing power embedded in deeply integrated B2B financial software.

thesisParagraphs: Corpay has historically been viewed primarily as a provider of specialized fuel cards for commercial fleets. While this core business remains highly profitable and incredibly sticky due to deep integration with corporate accounting software, the true value proposition lies in its evolution.

The strategic rebranding to Corpay reflects a successful pivot toward capturing a larger share of the massive B2B payments market. By leveraging its existing relationships and cross-selling AP automation, virtual cards, and cross-border payment solutions, Corpay is aggressively expanding its total addressable market while maintaining the high margins characteristic of closed-loop payment networks.
verdictFooter: Based on current market conditions. Data via Finviz/SEC EDGAR (Mar 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 9% growth rate for Corpay?
faq.answer: The 9% growth assumption relies on Corpay successfully migrating beyond legacy fleet cards and capturing significant market share in broader, highly fragmented corporate payment solutions like Accounts Payable automation.

faq.question: What is Corpay's main competitive advantage?
faq.answer: Corpay's primary advantage is high switching costs. Once its expense management and payment routing systems are embedded in a company's ERP software, it is operationally painful and expensive to switch providers.

faq.question: Is Corpay a good value at current prices?
faq.answer: According to our DCF valuation, Corpay presents an attractive opportunity, trading at a discount to its intrinsic value, making it an appealing prospect for investors seeking growth in the B2B fintech space.
